pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly stated, but implied as outer_radius - inner_radius = 1800 - 1000 = 800mm
pi_items.source: Geometric calculation: outer_radius (1800mm) - inner_radius (1000mm) = 800mm. Verified by verifier.py (line 132: checks roller length within 100mm of 800: 'if abs(roller["length"] - 800) > 100')
Task description (line 5: '**Center**: Origin (0,0)'). Verified by verifier.py (lines 90-91: checks arc center is within 1.0 units of origin: 'is_centered = abs(cx) < 1.0 and abs(cy) < 1.0')
Task description (line 5: '**Units**: Millimeters'). Confirmed by radius values (1000mm, 1800mm) and tolerance checks in verifier.py

privileged_info_summary: This task creates a synthetic 2D engineering drawing of a 90-degree curved roller conveyor in LibreCAD. The output file is saved to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/conveyor_curve.dxf. The drawing contains three layers: RAILS (blue/color 5), ROLLERS (yellow/color 2), and NOTES (white/color 7). The geometry consists of two concentric arcs centered at the origin with inner radius 1000mm and outer radius 1800mm, spanning 90 degrees. Seven radial roller lines connect the inner to outer arc at 15-degree intervals (0°, 15°, 30°, 45°, 60°, 75°, 90°), each 800mm in length. The drawing also includes text labels 'R1000' and 'R1800' approximately 50mm in height.
